source: Analyst pre-call question list for the CAVA Q1 FY2026 earnings call. The `expected.note_models` values are the fund's own model — the variant view the copilot grounds management's claims against.
thesis: CAVA is an early-innings unit-growth compounder: a defensible Mediterranean category lead with best-in-class AUVs and ~25% restaurant-level margins, a long runway to ~1,000 units, and TRAFFIC-LED same-restaurant sales. The bet is that new-unit economics and comp quality hold as the footprint moves beyond the coastal core.

questions.pillar: A long, derisked runway to ~1,000 units
questions.question: How derisked is the FY26 unit-growth guide, and how deep is the signed development pipeline beyond the next twelve months?
complete_when: a unit-growth figure or net-new-restaurant count for the year
some read on signed-lease coverage or pipeline visibility

expected.note_models: ~17% unit growth (~65 net new), fully covered by a signed-lease pipeline
expected.our_view: Runway is real; watch that openings stay on cadence as they push inland
expected.watch: A thin or unsigned pipeline would slip the compounding story

questions.pillar: New-unit economics hold as they scale
questions.question: Are recent-vintage new units opening at the modeled AUV, and how are the 2023-24 cohorts maturing on restaurant-level margin?
complete_when: an AUV figure for recent new-unit classes (not just 'tracking well')
a year-one restaurant-level margin for the recent cohorts

expected.note_models: ~$2.6M new-unit AUV at ~20% year-one restaurant-level margin (ramping to ~25% by year two)
expected.our_view: New-unit economics underwrite the ~1,000-unit math; a vague answer is not enough
expected.watch: Opening below ~$2.6M AUV lowers the whole unit-growth NPV

questions.pillar: New-unit economics hold as they scale
questions.question: Can the fleet restaurant-level margin hold through wage inflation, the steak/protein mix shift, and new-unit drag?
complete_when: a restaurant-level margin figure or range for the fleet
a read on the wage / mix / new-unit-drag pressures

expected.our_view: Margin durability underpins the self-funding growth model
expected.watch: Sustained margin slippage breaks the self-funding case

questions.question: What is the traffic vs price/mix split in current same-restaurant sales, and is traffic still positive?
complete_when: an explicit traffic vs price/mix split for current comps
whether traffic is positive, flat, or negative

expected.our_view: Comp QUALITY is the differentiator; price-led comp is lower quality and signals less runway
expected.watch: If comp is price-led with flat traffic, the premium multiple is weaker than underwritten

questions.question: Where does the loyalty relaunch stand, what is the digital sales mix, and what frequency lift are you seeing?
complete_when: a read on the loyalty relaunch / program status

expected.note_models: Digital ~35% of sales; loyalty relaunch driving frequency (the cleaner, traffic lever)
expected.our_view: Loyalty + digital are the durable traffic levers behind comp quality
expected.watch: Weak frequency lift undercuts the traffic-led comp thesis

questions.pillar: A defensible category lead, with optionality
questions.question: How defensible is the Mediterranean category lead versus Chipotle, Sweetgreen, and new entrants, and how much pricing power is left?
complete_when: a read on competitive positioning / category lead
a view on remaining pricing power

expected.note_models: Category-defining lead with durable pricing power
expected.our_view: Moat and pricing power determine whether the AUV/margin premium persists
expected.watch: Pricing power near its limit makes price-led comp non-repeatable

questions.pillar: A defensible category lead, with optionality
questions.question: What is the read on the catering rollout and any smaller-format or new-daypart prototype economics?
complete_when: a read on catering and/or new-format / new-daypart tests

expected.note_models: Optionality (catering, new formats) as incremental layers, not yet in numbers
expected.our_view: Free optionality on the growth algorithm

questions.pillar: A long, derisked runway to ~1,000 units
questions.question: Is the unit growth self-funded from operating cash flow, or is a raise needed, and is there any M&A or buyback appetite?
complete_when: whether growth is self-funded or needs external capital
any read on M&A / buyback appetite

expected.note_models: Self-funded from operating cash flow; clean balance sheet; reinvesting in units
expected.our_view: Funding risk: dilution or a stretched balance sheet changes the return profile
expected.watch: An equity raise would re-rate the return profile
